methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Velo3D is a public-stage company in Robotics & Manufacturing that has raised over $300M. The valuation landscape for this sector comprises a sparse comp universe: one public peer (277810.KQ) and eight private companies, including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics. This limited set reflects the nascent and heterogeneous nature of advanced manufacturing robotics.

The peer-median triangulation method is not applicable to this valuation. The Robotics & Manufacturing sector contains 11 same-sector peers overall, but filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier comparables yielded fewer than three datapoints in each narrow category—below the minimum threshold for reliable statistical inference. A fallback to all-sector median would obscure material differences in business models, maturity, and capital intensity across the peer set and introduce unacceptable bias.

This analysis cannot support a definitive valuation range without additional applicable triangulation methods or access to financial metrics (revenue, growth rate, margin trajectory) from the identified private peers and the single public comparable. The primary uncertainty stems from the thinness of the comp universe and the absence of standardized, comparable financial data across companies at different stages of commercialization. Future valuation work should focus on gathering detailed financial data from the five named private peers and analyzing the public peer's trading multiples.
